steps.title: Find the cause-and-effect clue
steps.content: The sentence reads: “Employing robots instead of humans will be helpful, as ______ astronauts to maintain the telescope can be expensive.” The word as signals a cause-and-effect relationship. The blank must explain what makes using humans expensive—the reason robots are helpful.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Relying on astronauts” completes the causal chain: this phrase describes depending on humans for maintenance work, and the sentence establishes that this dependency is expensive, which justifies using robots instead.

The other three either don't relate to cost (straightening), reverse the necessary logic (forgetting about), or create unclear phrasing (reducing).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
